title: FRIDGE TOUR with Paul Saladino MD

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The host introduces the contents of their fridge, highlighting a variety of animal-based foods. They emphasize the quality of the meat, particularly grass-fed options, and mention their affinity for raw dairy and other nutritious foods.
chunks.transcript: there it is what is up you guys I'm going to show you Insider access let me show you what is inside on the top shelf we have a huge grass-fed cow femur bone that I'm going to cut into get the marrow out and then cut the ends up and make bone broth with it behind that I've got grass-fed ground beef it's 80 20. that is the fattiest ground beef that I can find it's from a grass-fed operation here in Costa Rica that feeds real grass year round they don't use pesticides or herbicides on their farm so I know that it's good quality some of you guys think that I eat steaks every day 95 of what I eat is ground beef it's affordable it makes Burgers which I can dress up with all sorts of good things like grilled plantain butter sour cream as you'll see so I got the stash grass-fed ground beef right there thawing here I've got some raw sour cream and some raw butter that

chunks.title: Benefits of Raw Dairy and Bone Broth
chunks.summary: The video discusses the health benefits of consuming raw dairy, including lower rates of asthma and allergies in children. The host explains the preparation of bone broth and emphasizes the nutritional advantages of undenatured whey protein.
chunks.transcript: fan of raw dairy we know that when you heat milk in the pasture is Asian process above 65 degrees Centigrade or about 160 degrees Fahrenheit the whey protein is changed we also know that kids who grow up on or off Farms drinking raw dairy raw milk have lower rates of asthma allergies and eczema so it appears that this undenatured whey protein is probably beneficial for humans from an immunologic perspective so I try to get my dairy in a way that hasn't broken hasn't denatured that whey protein here I've got raw testicles that are thawing what fridge would be complete without some raw testicles I've got some raisins and dates I like the dried fruit for Boron I've got some coconuts down there I'm in Costa Rica so I drink those after surf there's some bone broth that I made myself from grass-fed beef bones and reverse osmosis water raw milk here I've got some ghee that's clarified butter from the same Farm in Costa Rica that I like some Tallow if I want extra fat some raw cheese raw cheese is great because even if raw dairy is not available where you are you can usually find raw cheese how do you know if rod is available where you are you can go to realmilk.com because I like butter so much I've always got extra stashes of raw butter from grass-fed cows locally here in Costa Rica go into the freezer it's time for a restock but it's okay I've got

chunks.title: Utilizing Nutrient-Dense Foods
chunks.summary: The speaker showcases a variety of nutrient-dense animal products, such as raw organs and ghee, and discusses their importance in an animal-based diet. They also mention sourcing practices for these items to ensure quality.

chunks.title: Choosing Clean Honey
chunks.summary: The host explains their criteria for selecting honey, focusing on sourcing from areas free from pesticides. They mention receiving reports that confirm the absence of harmful chemicals in the honey they use.
chunks.transcript: whole package beef organs got some maple syrup because I like that occasionally to switch up from the honey low microplastic sea salt an assortment of local low glyphosate Honeys when I'm selecting honey I think a lot about where the honey is produced are there fields are there farms where they're using pesticides within six miles of the honey in Costa Rica one of the nice things is there are a lot of places where you can get honey from way up in the mountains like this Farm or this Farm or this Farm or this Farm where there's no glyphosate around one of the cool things this company here in Costa Rica actually sent me a report of all of the chemicals they tested the honey for there were no pesticides no other herbicides no fungicides in the whole honey nothing was detected so it's awesome that they did this here in Costa Rica for me super clean honey are what

chunks.title: Pantry and Fruit Selections
chunks.summary: An overview of the host's pantry follows, detailing the use of glass for food storage and the preparation of homemade kefir. Additionally, they discuss the importance of incorporating low-toxic plant foods and various fruit options.
chunks.transcript: because I only store my food in glass I don't want to store my food in plastic on the bottom shelf I've got my Vitamix with stainless steel blender so that's amazing too that's my whole Pantry here I've got some kefir that I make myself to do that I use kefir grains as a starter take the raw milk from the fridge and I mix it in here and within a day or so it ferments into kefir a little more probiotic gets rid of some of that lactose because I'm definitely lactose intolerant and what goes well with meat and organs and raw dairy and honey some fruit the least toxic plant Foods we've got the upside down pineapples there's nothing to do with being a swinger I'm not a swinger it just makes them much more juicy and sweet when you store them upside down I need a bit of a fruit resupply soon too but I've got a local organic papaya this is a black pudding sapote that I'm waiting to ripen some grow Michel bananas which are grown here in Costa Rica they're some of the best I've ever had it's got a plantain that's going to go on the grill that one is perfectly ready to get grilled tonight so that plantain is going to get grilled with some butter maybe some sour cream and

chunks.title: Conclusion on Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The host wraps up by reinforcing their belief in the animal-based diet as the most evolutionarily appropriate way for humans to eat, highlighting the consumption of meat, organs, fruit, honey, and raw dairy as essential elements.
chunks.transcript: here because there's a cabinet here with all kinds of that we use for reels those are like the ghosts from Ghostbusters we keep them in there with the protector spirits machine yeah you guys have seen the stuff in there but don't actually eat that just in there for the reels and stuff that we film about it thanks for joining me on this tour of my fridge my pantry my fruit stash my fermented milk this is how we do it animal-based diet I'm going to cut into this bone later today and share some of this Rich bone marrow with my friends then I'm gonna make some broth with the bones and all the cartilage hanging off there an animal-based diet I strongly believe this is the most evolutionarily appropriate way for humans to eat what do our ancestors seek Above All Things meat and organs they eat the animal from nose to tail if our ancestors are going to eat plant Foods what are they going to go for first things that are colorful and sweet that's fruit less of the plants defense chemicals in there they're also definitely going to eat honey if they can find it select glyphosate free honey and then more recently raw dairy in the form of raw butter Frost sour cream raw milk and kefir are a delicious nutritious addition to the human diet this is an animal-based diet organs meat fruit honey and raw dairy super easy def definitely affordable wherever you are
